zh.Tyle Hall, Latchingdon, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可拉欽格頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Richard Osborne於1468年出生在英國艾可拉欽格頓時，他的父親Sir Peter Osborne28歲，母親Emme Bourchier24歲。他於1487年與Lady Wilhelmina Richards在英國肯特阿福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1530年在英國肯特阿福德享年62歲。

zh-hans.Tyle Hall, Latchingdon, Essex, England: 英国英格兰艾可拉欽格頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Richard Osborne于1468年出生在英国艾可拉欽格頓时，他的父亲Sir Peter Osborne28岁，母亲Emme Bourchier24岁。他于1487年与Lady Wilhelmina Richards在英国肯特阿福德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1530年在英国肯特阿福德享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Osborne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Osbern, partly from late Old English Ōsbern, an Anglicized form of Old Norse Ásbiǫrn (from áss ‘god’ + biǫrn ‘bear’), but more usually from Norman use of the cognate ancient Germanic Ōsbern. Osbern was often treated as an alternative form of Osbert. For pet forms see Hoskin and Opie . The surname has also been established in Ireland (Waterford, Tipperary) since the mid 16th century.

variants.name: Oborne
meanings: habitational name from Oborne in Dorset. The placename derives from Old English wōh ‘twisted, crooked’ + burna ‘spring, stream’.
possibly from the Middle English personal name Obern, an Anglo-Norman French form of Osbern (see Osborne ).

variants.name: Hoskin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Osekin with prosthetic H-, a pet form of Anglo-Norman French names beginning with Os- (particularly Osbert, as well as Osbern, and Osmund) + the Flemish diminutive suffix -kin. All three personal names were current in medieval Cornwall or Devon; see Osborne , Osmond , and Opie (a pet form of Osbert). Old English Ōsbeorn, Ōsbeohrt, and Ōsmund may also have contributed in a minor way to Middle English usage; Middle English Oswald is another possible source, but the name was very rare in the late 14th century. For the excrescent initial H-, compare Herrick .

variants.name: Oborn
meanings: English (Somerset): habitational name chiefly from Oborne in Dorset, or perhaps occasionally from Howbourne Farm in Hadlow Down, Sussex, Woburn in Bedfordshire, Woburn Park in Surrey, or Wooburn in Buckinghamshire. The placenames derive from Old English wōh ‘twisted, crooked’ + burna ‘spring, stream’, except Wooburn in Buckinghamshire, which is of uncertain etymology.
English (of Norman origin): perhaps from the Middle English personal name Obern, an Anglo-Norman French form of Osbern (see Osborne ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Richard Osborne was born in 1468, in Latchingdon, Essex, England, his father, Sir Peter Osborne, was 28 and his mother, Emme Bourchier, was 24. He married Lady Wilhelmina Richards in 1487, in Ashford, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1530, in Ashford, Kent, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Richard Osborne est né en 1468, à Latchingdon, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Peter Osborne, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Emme Bourchier, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Lady Wilhelmina Richards en 1487, à Ashford, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1530, à Ashford, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Richard Osborne 1468 in Latchingdon, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Peter Osborne 28 und seine Mutter Emme Bourchier 24. Er hat 1487 in Ashford, Kent, England Lady Wilhelmina Richards geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1530 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Ashford, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Richard Osborne è nato il nel 1468 nel Latchingdon, Essex, England, il suo padre, Sir Peter Osborne, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Emme Bourchier, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Lady Wilhelmina Richards nel 1487, nel Ashford, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1530, nel Ashford, Kent, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Richard Osborneが1468年にイギリスエセックスラトチングドンで生まれた時，父Sir Peter Osborneは28歳，母Emme Bourchierは24歳であった。1487年にイギリスケントアシュフォードでLady Wilhelmina Richardsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1530年にイギリスケントアシュフォードで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Richard Osborne(이)가 1468년 영국 에세스 라트친그던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Peter Osborne(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Emme Bourchier(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1487년 영국 켄트 애스h퍼드에서 Lady Wilhelmina Richards(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1530년 영국 켄트 애스h퍼드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Richard Osborne nasceu em 1468, em Latchingdon, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Peter Osborne, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Emme Bourchier, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Wilhelmina Richards em 1487, em Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1530, em Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Richard Osborne родился в 1468 г. в местности лачингдон, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Peter Osborne было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Emme Bourchier, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Wilhelmina Richards в 1487 г. в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1530 г. в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Richard Osborne nació en 1468, en Latchingdon, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Peter Osborne, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Emme Bourchier, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Lady Wilhelmina Richards en 1487, en Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1530, en Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
